contaminant.name: Soap Scum Buildup
contaminant.description: Soap-scum contamination, it manifests as fatty acid salt deposits that arise from the interaction between soap residues and hard water minerals, which tenaciously adhere to surfaces in humid environments. This layer, it distinguishes itself from mineral scales or organic films through its waxy, translucent consistency and sensitivity to pH variations, behaving differently on materials like tile or glass where it spreads unevenly and resists mechanical abrasion, yet persists less stubbornly on ceramics compared to metals. Key challenges for removal include its strong bonding influenced from repeated exposure, but laser cleaning proves effective, as the process selectively ablates the scum without damaging the substrate, leading to efficient restoration.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Formaldehyde and Carbon monoxide generation - multiple toxic compounds
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
